text:
a progression of lesions over time. Conversely,
the presence of primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC)
appears to be associated with a lower rate of surgery.
The risk of developing colorectal cancer increases
in the presence of PSC, with prolonged disease duration (beyond
10 years), in males, and when diagnosed at a young age.
It is important to note that an initial localization
limited to the rectum does not guarantee a benign course;
rapid extension of lesions can occur and lead to
subsequent colectomy (46,47).
Nutritional impact, linear growth,
pubertal development,
final height and cancer
Nutritional impact
Weight loss is an almost constant sign
at the time of diagnosis of IBD in children, with weight
below the 3rd percentile or at -2 standard deviations (SD)
in about 50% of cases. A North American study
including 783 children reported a body mass index
(BMI) below the 5th percentile in 23% of patients
with CD, compared with 8% in those with UC (9).
Linear growth and pubertal development
Growth delay, d
